# caveui

> caveui is a community-driven catalog of beautiful, copy-friendly Jetpack Compose components for Android. Inspired by shadcn/ui, uiverse and Tailwind UI — but built on Material 3. There is no black box: every component is source you read, copy, and own. The monorepo holds the Kotlin/Gradle library, an Android sample app, and a static Next.js docs/gallery site.

Repository: https://github.com/Jumnert/CaveUI

## Structure

- [README](https://github.com/Jumnert/CaveUI/blob/main/README.md): Project overview, repo layout, quick start, and how to run/deploy the docs site.
- [CONTRIBUTING](https://github.com/Jumnert/CaveUI/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md): Step-by-step workflow for adding a component (the `Button` component is the reference template).
- [LICENSE](https://github.com/Jumnert/CaveUI/blob/main/LICENSE): MIT license.

## Library (Jetpack Compose, Kotlin/Gradle)

- `library/src/main/java/io/caveui/theme/`: Design tokens — Color.kt, Type.kt, Shape.kt, Spacing.kt, Theme.kt. `CaveTheme` builds on Material 3 for free dark mode and dynamic color.
- `library/src/main/java/io/caveui/components/`: One folder per component (e.g. `button/`). Each component is self-contained.
- `sample/`: Android demo app — the test bed for every component.

## Docs site (Next.js)

- `web/`: Static Next.js + Tailwind + MDX gallery (no backend, `output: "export"`). See web/llms.txt for site details.
- `web/src/lib/registry/`: Single source of truth for the gallery, detail pages, and CLI install commands — one file per category.

## Usage

```kotlin
import io.caveui.components.button.CaveButton
import io.caveui.theme.CaveTheme

CaveTheme {
    CaveButton(text = "Get started", onClick = { /* ... */ })
}
```

Add a component with the CLI: `npx caveui@latest add <id>` (e.g. `button-gradient`).

## Build & verify

- Android library: `./gradlew assembleDebug`
- Docs site: `cd web && npm install && npm run build`
